card.intuition: A distributed streaming platform that treats messages as a distributed, immutable log of events, allowing for high throughput and fault tolerance.
card.analogy: A digital continuous scroll (like a never-ending receipt printer): messages are appended to the end of the roll. Different readers (Consumers) can read from any point on the roll using a 'bookmark' (Offset).
card.time_complexity: O(1) to append and read by offset
when_to_use: High-throughput real-time data pipelines (billions of events/day)
gotchas: Kafka is NOT a simple task queue; messages aren't 'deleted' immediately after being read (they are kept based on retention time/size)
Misconfiguring 'Partitions' can lead to data being processed in the wrong order or unevenly across servers

exercise.prompt: Identify the Kafka component that allows for horizontal scaling of a single Topic.
exercise.starter_code:
def identify_kafka_scaling():
    # Dividing a topic into multiple pieces across brokers
    component = "__________"
    return component
hints: It starts with 'P' and means a part of a whole
